title: crucianella gilanica subsp. glauca
intro: Crucianella gilanica subsp. glauca, also known as a type of shrub in the Lamiaceae family, is native to the mountainous regions of Iran and has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. The plant contains compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable medicinal resource. Its health benefits include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and acting as an antiseptic agent to combat infections. Herbal preparations of Crucianella gilanica subsp. glauca can be made into infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be consumed as teas or taken as supplements to harness its medicinal properties.
